_README: Copy this file to ground_truth.json and fill in real entries keyed by photo filename (as it appears in eval/photos/). Every field is OPTIONAL — only include the axes you have a confident judgment about for that photo. Omitted fields are skipped, not scored as failures.

_schema.on_power_point: bool — does the main subject sit near a rule-of-thirds intersection?
_schema.has_leading_lines: bool — are there real compositional leading lines (not texture/foliage clutter)?
_schema.expected_dominant_angle_deg: number, optional — the real line's angle in degrees [0,180). Catches 'right boolean, wrong reason' (e.g. detector says True because it found foliage clutter, not your actual staircase/architecture line).
_schema.angle_tolerance_deg: number, optional, default 20 — how close actual dominant_angle must be to count as a match
_schema.is_symmetric: bool — would a human call this photo symmetric (mirror-like)?
_schema.dominant_axis: 'vertical' | 'horizontal' — only checked if is_symmetric is true. 'vertical' = left/right mirror, 'horizontal' = top/bottom mirror.
_schema.has_horizon: bool — is there a visible horizon line in the frame?
_schema.has_significant_negative_space: bool — is there substantial empty/breathing-room space around the subject?
_schema.expected_region: 'center' | 'top-left' | 'top-center' | 'top-right' | 'middle-left' | 'middle-right' | 'bottom-left' | 'bottom-center' | 'bottom-right' — where YOU judge the subject to sit in a 3x3 grid
